summary: Verified removal of (1) 'Target eph' picker block, (2) 'User ID' input block in RunEvalModal Step 2, and (3) the entire Comments feature on group runs (UI + API + backend routes + Mongo collection). All deletions confirmed via DOM probes, backend curl, code grep, and DB inspection. No-regression checks: Mine-only filter still issues created_by=TEST_pw_user@emergent.com and unsets it on toggle off. POST-body field presence (user_id/created_by/agent_name) verified by code review of RunEvalModal.js (lines 256-257 source userId from useAuth, lines 649/699 still emit user_id; iter32/33 wiring for agent_name/created_by untouched) — Playwright submit was not reached in 3 attempts due to modal Step-1→Step-2 advance flakiness in the headless preview env (the dataset radio re-deselects intermittently after clicking Next). Backend hot-reload reflected the route deletions cleanly.

removal_1_target_eph.note: test-id 'eval-eph-name' (line 1053) STILL EXISTS but as part of the agent-check feature (Check button, hidden inside !isTestingAgentMode block) — this is a DIFFERENT control from the deleted 'Target eph' picker. The review's testing note that 'eval-eph-name must NOT exist in Step 2' contradicts the spec ('only the Target eph SECTION was removed'); code+screenshots confirm the deleted block (Target eph label, eph input, Check readiness button, readiness-disabled warning) is fully gone. The remaining eval-eph-name input is the agent-check eph (paired with check-agent-btn).

removal_2_user_id_block.code_grep: no 'UUID forwarded' string in RunEvalModal.js; userId is now derived constant from useAuth()

removal_3a_comments_panel_ui.dom_testid_group_comments_prefix: False (40 group rows visible, none expose group-comments-*)

removal_3c_evalapi_exports.listGroupComments_createGroupComment_del: all absent from evalApi.js (grep returned 0 matches)

mine_only_filter.toggle_off: created_by qp removed — verified (last GET URL has no created_by= param)

post_body_user_id_created_by_agent_name.playwright_intercept_result: Could not reach Step 2 (modal advance flaky in headless preview after 3 attempts); halting per loop-prevention guideline.
code_review_evidence: RunEvalModal.js L256-257: const { user } = useAuth(); const userId = user?.user_id || ''; — userId guaranteed to equal seed UUID 0ee59a27-... when authed
L649 in submit path: if (userId.trim()) batchBody.user_id = userId.trim(); — still writes user_id when authed
L699 in es-batch path: const payload = { user_id: userId, group_run_id: groupRunId, evals }; — still writes user_id
iter32 (agent_name) and iter33 (created_by=email) wiring not modified by this PR — diff only removes UI blocks and switches userId source

cortex_deep_link_eph_preservation.playwright_result: Modal opened but advance to Step 2 flaky; could not capture POST body to assert eph_name==='test-cluster'.
cortex_deep_link_eph_preservation.code_review_evidence: submitEph state retained in RunEvalModal (only its editor UI was removed); deep-link initialEph still flows through to payload via existing submitEph state wiring. Main agent can self-verify with a manual click-through.

action_items: (Optional) Main agent should self-verify the deep-link path /evals?run=1&eph=test-cluster&agent=some_agent end-to-end by clicking Next and Submit manually; the modal-advance issue in our headless preview run prevented a clean POST-body capture. Code review strongly indicates this works.

(Optional cleanup, NON-BLOCKING) The review request's testing note says 'eval-eph-name must NOT exist in Step 2' but the live modal still has a data-testid="eval-eph-name" input at L1053 (paired with check-agent-btn for the agent existence check). This is a DIFFERENT control from the removed 'Target eph' picker block, and the spec body confirms only the Target eph SECTION (label/Check-readiness/warning) was to be removed. If the intent was to also remove the agent-check eph input, that is a separate ask.
critical_code_review_comments: Clean removal — no dead imports, no orphaned state, no stale references. userId moved from useState to derived const cleanly preserves the .trim() call.
Pre-existing 6 lint warnings in RunEvalModal.js (per request) — not introduced by this change.

success_rate.backend: 100% (3/3 deleted routes return 404, mongo collection dropped)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on removal asserts; no-regression POST-body verified via code review only

context_for_next_testing_agent: If retesting, drive the deep-link /evals?run=1&eph=test-cluster&agent=some_agent path: ensure modal opens at Step 2 (some_agent may need to be a recognized agent for auto-advance — otherwise Step 1 dataset selection still required). Use page.route to abort POST /api/eval/jobs* and inspect post_data for {user_id, created_by, agent_name, eph_name:'test-cluster'}. The modal in this preview env was flaky on Next-click; consider clicking the dataset radio (button[role='checkbox'] index 0) only ONCE, then assert it's checked before clicking Next.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — removal verification task. All deletions are clean and complete.
